pmos /magazine pipeline robustness — epic design (260624-2bm)
Created using pmos‑learnkit

/magazine pipeline robustness — epic design (260624-2bm)

Context

A real /magazine "catch me up on last week" run shipped a valid 59-item issue + a 3-issue library with the trust rule honored (16 honestly-degraded cards, zero fabrication) — but the path required three hand-rolled workarounds. A retro surfaced five findings + one feature request, all confirmed live in the shipped scripts. This epic (260624-2bm, route: skill, target pmos-learnkit/magazine) closes them across two serialized stories. The full triage (parsed findings, critique, dispositions, per-skill tier) is the source: doc 0c_feedback_triage.html.

This is the epic design page — the cross-skill coherence contract the per-story plans cite by anchor (there is no epic-level /spec in a route: skill define; the skill spec folds into each story's /plan).

Pre-flight — grounded against live source (2026-06-24)

All paths under plugins/pmos-learnkit/skills/magazine/. The shipped scripts are pure zero-dep Node (≥18); tests are bash integration (tests/structure.test.sh, bundles.test.sh, watch.test.sh) + in-file --selftest harnesses. None of the six findings was silently fixed in 0.16.0.

  • #1 windowing: parseOpts (magazine-run.js:423-442) recognizes --feed, --feeds, --since, --max, --root, --min-chars, --backfill, --budget-sec — no --days. The loop has no else branch, so an unrecognized flag is silently dropped (exit 0). cmdDiscover passes one global opts.since to every feed (:129); when null, fetch-feed.js applies no lower bound → full history. SKILL.md Phase 2 (:152-160) + pipeline.md §Windowing describe a --days lookback the entrypoint cannot consume and never tell the runner to convert it to ISO --since.
  • #2 prep scope: cmdPrep loops the entire ledger — for (const guid of Object.keys(st.items)) { … if (item.status !== 'discovered') continue; … } (:167-169). The discover snapshot returned at :140 is never persisted or consulted; no run-id scoping exists.
  • #3 Stage-B GUID: render-issue.js does no merge — it consumes an agent-assembled items.json (:421-422; reads it.bullets at :204). The drop happens in the Phase-4 agent layer: safeGuid (magazine-run.js:42-44) maps [^A-Za-z0-9._-]→_ but only for on-disk cache/transcript filenames; nothing re-keys summarizer output back to manifest GUIDs.
  • #4 durable items: no script persists a per-issue *_items.json; render-issue.js issue writes only HTML to stdout. renderLibrary consumes {issues:[{date,file,items:[{title,feed,tags,date,link}]}]} (:292-308) but nothing builds it from prior issues. Cards carry data-guid (:215) but library rows don't, and the oldest issue HTML predates data-guid.
  • #5 feed health: state schema is {cursors, items, links} (config-schema.md:108-126; magazine-state.js) — no per-feed failure field. fetchOne catches, writes one stderr line, returns [] every run (:100-107). The only counter is per-podcast-item transcription (MAX_TRANSCRIBE_ATTEMPTS = 3, :254) — item-level, not feed-level.
  • #6 date range: only a lower bound (--since) + count cap (--max) exist. No upper bound in parseOpts, fetchOne (:94-99 forwards only --since/--max), or fetch-feed.js. The issue HTML reader's f-from/f-to (render-issue.js:143-144) is post-render UI filtering, not a crawl-time window. The lower bound is applied inside fetch-feed.js — the natural hook for an upper bound too. Same call-site as #1.
  • Cross-run reuse (basis for D6): state.discover is idempotent — if (state.items[guid]) return false (magazine-state.js:105) — so a re-run never resurrects an already-rendered item. prep skips non-discovered items, and crawl text is content-cached at crawl-cache/<safe-guid>.txt (:193). So crawl + transcribe are already reused across overlapping windows. But item takeaways are NOT stored in state.json (the item record has no bullets field) — they live only in the ephemeral items.json + rendered HTML, so summary reuse today requires scraping prior issue HTML. The durable per-issue items JSON (#4) is the missing reuse substrate.

Goal & non-goals

Goal: make a /magazine build robust and predictable — the window the user asks for is the window discovered; prep can't balloon; no item is silently dropped between Stage B and render; the library survives a rebuild; persistently-dead feeds get a nudge; and an overlapping/wider window reuses prior work instead of redoing it. The trust rule is unchanged and load-bearing throughout.

Non-goals: auto-disabling a feed (suggest-only, D3); a crawl-time content filter beyond date bounds; reworking the issue HTML reader's existing client-side date UI; changing the two-stage pipeline shape, the closed tag registry, the watch enqueue/drain + forward-only cursor contract (unchanged — D7 scopes the cursor to watch, it does not alter watch's own behavior), or the issue/library HTML contract; building a multi-snapshot store or a sidecar-backfill migration (D2/D8 explicitly defer both); speed (explicitly not a goal for this skill — correctness + reuse are).

Decisions

IDDecisionRationale
D1Native window flags on discover: parse --from <date>/--to <date> (and the ISO --since/--until equivalents) for an explicit window, plus --days N sugar for a trailing window; resolve a lower bound and an optional upper bound; reject unknown flags loudly (non-zero + stderr). Interactive discover requires a window (see D7).Unifies #1 + #6 at one call-site. The silent-drop is what hid #1, so failing loud on an unknown flag is part of the fix, not a nicety.
D2Persist the discover snapshot under a run-id; scope prep to it. run-id = the discover's ISO timestamp; exactly one snapshot is retained, overwritten by each discover (no list, no GC). prep always scopes to that single snapshot. --snapshot <id> stays a documented hook but is inert until a multi-snapshot need appears (YAGNI).Gives "this run = these items" a durable boundary so a bad discover can't make prep crawl the whole ledger. Single-latest matches the one-issue-per-discover flow and needs no retention machinery (grill).
D3Feed quarantine is suggest-only. state.feedHealth[slug].consecFails increments on each run where the feed fetch fails and resets to 0 on any successful fetch ("consecutive runs" is the unit even when runs are days apart). At ≥ N=3 (named constant), discover/prep prints one compact suggestion line per run; never auto-disable.Auto-disabling would silently stop trying a feed — in tension with the no-silent-drop trust rule. Reset-on-success + one compact line avoids a nag-block while keeping the signal visible (grill).
D4GUID reconciliation = verbatim echo + normalized fallback. Phase 4 instructs summarizers to echo the source GUID verbatim (opaque); render reconciles by normalizing both keys via the shared safeGuid transform before matching.Belt-and-suspenders: the echo prevents the safe-ify in the first place; the normalized fallback recovers any that still drift. One shared transform avoids divergence.
D5Durable per-issue items JSON. render-issue.js issue persists {YYYY-MM-DD}_items.json next to the issue; library assembles issues.json by globbing those sidecars, not by parsing HTML.Closes #4: the library rebuild stops reverse-engineering rendered HTML, and the sidecar becomes the reuse substrate for D6.
D6Overlapping-window reuse is a first-class goal. A wider/overlapping window reuses already-crawled/transcribed/summarized work and re-processes only genuinely-new items. The snapshot includes in-window already-processed items (they render from cache / persisted bullets); prep-scoping bounds only what is crawled.Surfaced by the maintainer's monthly-vs-weekly question. Crawl+transcribe reuse already holds; summary reuse is unlocked by D5's sidecar. Naming it prevents the builder from treating reuse as accidental.
D7Explicit window is the interactive interface; the cursor goes private (grill). Interactive discover requires a window (--from/--to or --days); bare no-arg discover errors with a usage message demanding a window (no more silent full-history pull). --since is deprecated/hidden. The per-feed cursor is retained but user-invisible — it survives only as the timer-driven watch enqueue/drain "new since last poll" memory (a human can't pass dates to a scheduler).Maintainer challenge: "is the cursor even needed? require start+end dates." Right for the interactive path (kills #1 at the root, simpler mental model) but the cursor is load-bearing for unattended watch (verified at magazine-watch.js:255-258), so it is scoped to watch rather than deleted.
D8No sidecar backfill; new issues only (grill). The library-from-sidecars rebuild does not backfill missing sidecars and retains no legacy HTML parsers. Issues built before this ships have no *_items.json and drop out of the library on the first rebuild — but the rebuild emits one loud notice naming the skipped issues (no silent drop; rebuild them to re-include).Maintainer chose the simplest steady-state code over a one-time migration; the loud notice reconciles "accept the drop" with the skill's own no-silent-drop trust rule (grill).

Invariants (cross-skill coherence contract)

  • Inv-1 — the trust rule is untouched. Every card is grounded in a real crawl/transcript or visibly degraded with an honest reason. No fix may introduce a fabricated, invented, or silently-dropped item (D3 quarantine is suggest-only precisely for this).
  • Inv-2 — one canonical key. The item GUID is the single ledger/cache/snapshot key; the safeGuid transform has exactly one home and is reused for filename-safety and reconciliation alike (D4). Cursors/snapshots key by feed slug, not URL (the existing FR-R4 contract).
  • Inv-3 — zero runtime dependencies. All scripts stay pure Node ≥18, no package.json, no npm. New code is selftested in-file + via tests/structure.test.sh.
  • Inv-4 — explicit-window interactive, watch-internal cursor, graceful state absence (D7/D8). Interactive discover requires a window and errors loudly with none — it does not silently fall back to a cursor (that fallback is what hid #1). The cursor is not a user-facing interactive concept; it persists only as watch's forward-only poll memory, and the watch enqueue/drain contract (incl. FR-7/FR-R4 invariants + tests) is unchanged. State-absence is still tolerated: absent a snapshot, prep falls back to current behavior; absent per-issue sidecars, library degrades with a loud skip-notice (does not crash). No state migration is forced on existing users.
  • Inv-5 — issue/library HTML contract preserved. reference/issue-format.md (card shape, data-guid, library row schema, client-side filters) is unchanged; D5 adds a sidecar, it does not alter the emitted HTML schema.

Functional requirements

Story A — windowing + prep snapshot-scoping (#1, #2, #6)

  • FR-1.1 (D1, D7) discover parses an explicit window — --from <date>/--to <date> and the ISO --since <ISO>/--until <ISO> equivalents — plus --days N sugar (trailing window = now − N days .. now). Lower-bound precedence on the interactive path: explicit --from/--since > --days. There is no cursor or unbounded fallback on the interactive path.
  • FR-1.2 parseOpts exits non-zero with a stderr message naming any unrecognized ---prefixed flag. The four new window flags register in the same shared parseOpts so loud-reject breaks no existing verb (enqueue/drain/status caller flags are all already known — verified).
  • FR-1.3 (D7) bare no-arg interactive discover errors with a usage message demanding a window (exit non-zero) instead of pulling full history. --since is accepted but deprecated/hidden (not advertised in argument-hint or docs).
  • FR-1.4 SKILL.md Phase 2/3 + pipeline.md §Windowing rewritten to the real flag surface (window-required, --from/--to/--days); argument-hint updated; no "convert --days→--since by hand" instruction remains; the cursor is documented as watch-internal only.
  • FR-2.1 (D2) discover writes this run's item set under a run-id (= the discover's ISO timestamp) as the single state.snapshot in state.json, overwriting any prior snapshot.
  • FR-2.2 (D2) prep scopes to that single snapshot; out-of-snapshot discovered items are not crawled. --snapshot <id> is parsed-but-inert (documented hook, no multi-snapshot store yet).
  • FR-2.3 (D6) the snapshot includes in-window items already at summarized/rendered so a wider/overlapping window renders them from cache/persisted bullets; snapshot-scoping bounds only what prep crawls, never what the issue contains. (discover stays GUID-idempotent, so already-processed in-window items keep their advanced status.)
  • FR-6.1 / FR-6.2 fetch-feed.js applies the upper bound (today: lower only); range endpoints inclusive; ISO + bare-date parsing; both bounds selftested.

Story B — Stage-B reconciliation + durable items + feed health (#3, #4, #5)

  • FR-3.1 the safeGuid transform is exported/shared as the single normalization source.
  • FR-3.2 Phase 4 instructs verbatim-GUID echo + a normalized-key reconciliation fallback; selftested with a safe-ified-key fixture (no item drops).
  • FR-4.1 render-issue.js issue persists {YYYY-MM-DD}_items.json next to the issue HTML.
  • FR-4.2 (D8) library assembles issues.json by globbing the *_items.json sidecars (no HTML parsing, no legacy pre-data-guid parser retained); Phase 5 updated. Issues with no sidecar (those built before this ships) are skipped, not backfilled — and the rebuild emits one loud notice naming the skipped issues (no silent drop; rebuilding an issue re-creates its sidecar and re-includes it). Selftested: a fixture with a sidecar-less issue produces the notice and omits that issue without crashing.
  • FR-4.3 (D6) issue assembly reuses persisted takeaways for already-summarized items, re-summarizing only items with no cached summary; selftested with a mixed cached/new fixture.
  • FR-5.1 state.json tracks consecutive per-feed failure counts (reset on success); after N=3 (named constant), discover/prep surface a quarantine suggestion; never auto-disable.

Story split

Two serialized vertical slices on one skill, mirroring the established 1-skill-N-stories pattern. Each is independently shippable and scored once against skill-eval.md.

  • 260624-xck — windowing flags + date range + loud unknown-flag + prep snapshot-scoping (#1, #2, #6) — route: skill, no deps.
  • 260624-9fw — Stage-B GUID reconciliation + durable per-issue items JSON + reuse-on-render + feed-failure quarantine suggestion (#3, #4, #5) — route: skill, depends on 260624-xck.

B depends on A because both edit SKILL.md and the state.json schema; serializing avoids the collision and lets B build on A's snapshot/durable-intermediate groundwork (D2 ↔ D5/D6 share the "persist durable intermediates" mechanism). The D24 litmus holds: A is the discover/prep call-site cluster, B is the Stage-B/render/feed-health cluster — no task in one depends on a task in the other beyond the story-level SKILL.md/state ordering.

Risks

  • State-schema churn. Both stories add fields (single snapshot/run-id; per-feed feedHealth count). Inv-4's absence-tolerant defaults + the migration-free posture keep existing ledgers working; selftests must assert the no-field fallback path.
  • Interactive render's relationship to the now-private cursor (for /plan). D7 makes the cursor watch-internal, so interactive discover no longer reads it. Story A's /plan must decide whether the Phase-6 render still advances the per-feed cursor: it is only load-bearing for podcast feeds under watch (which advances its own cursor via enqueue), so the cleanest answer is that interactive render no longer touches the cursor and article-feed cursors become vestigial-but-harmless. Flagged here, resolved in plan — not silently assumed.
  • Sidecar drop is accepted (D8), made loud. Pre-ship issues without a sidecar drop from the library on first rebuild; the residual risk is purely UX (a user surprised their old issues vanished), mitigated by the FR-4.2 loud skip-notice and the "rebuild to re-include" instruction. No correctness/trust-rule violation (notice, not silent).
  • SKILL.md collision between stories. Mitigated by the B→A dependency; the build loop merges A's branch into B's worktree before B's skill-eval (claim-time transitive-closure merge).
pmos Source: docs/pmos/features/2026-06-24_magazine-pipeline-robustness/02_design.html · Created using pmos‑learnkit
